title: Default Reporting Column to Blank and Expand Dropdown Options in Dictionary
story: As a DataTools Pro user working with the Data Dictionary, I want newly generated records to have a blank Reporting column by default and additional classification options in the dropdown, so that I can accurately categorize fields without misleading defaults.
description:
When new Dictionary records are generated, the Reporting column should default to blank instead of a pre-filled value. The Reporting dropdown options are also expanded.

## Changes
1. **Default value**: Newly generated Dictionary records have a blank Reporting column
2. **Dropdown options expanded** to: (blank), Yes, No, Metric, KPI
3. **Label fix**: Dropdown label displays "Yes" with proper capitalization instead of lowercase "yes"
acceptance_criteria: Newly generated Dictionary records have a blank Reporting column by default
Reporting dropdown includes options: blank, Yes, No, Metric, KPI
Dropdown label for 'Yes' uses proper capitalization
Existing records with previously set Reporting values are not affected
Dropdown change saves correctly and updates the table
ux_changes: Reporting dropdown expanded with new options (Metric, KPI) and defaults to blank for new records. Label capitalization fixed from 'yes' to 'Yes'.
middleware_changes: Record generation logic updated to set Reporting column as blank by default instead of a pre-filled value.
data_model_changes: Reporting field default value changed to blank/null. New enum values added: Metric, KPI.
implementation_notes: Updated the record generation logic to default Reporting to blank. Added Metric and KPI as new dropdown options alongside existing Yes/No values. Fixed capitalization on the Yes label in the dropdown.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the blank default and new dropdown options, pushed to staging. Newly added objects default to blank Reporting. All enhancements delivered together.
